content.full_text:
City of Berkeley
Office of the City Attorney
Martin Luther King, Jr.
Civic Center Building
2180 Milvia Street
Berkeley, California 94704

Telephone: (510) 644-6380
Telecopy: (510) 644-8641

April 8, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "I" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Written Advice

Dear Colleagues:

We are writing to request your written advice concerning whether the City of Berkeley's proposed Executive Collateral Geographical Responsibility program would violate the Political Reform Act. Attached is a copy of a draft memo which describes the program.

As you can see, basically the program provides that executive employees of the City will be given the responsibility for reporting deficiencies, such as pot holes, over grown vegetation, etc., to the appropriate city department for corrective action. Each executive will be given a designated geographical area in the City, which will correspond to the location where the executive resides in the City. We are interested in knowing whether this program would result in the executive participating in the making of a governmental decision in which he or she may have a financial interest.

If you need further information or have any questions, please free to contact the undersigned.

Sincerely,

MANUELA ALBUQUERQUE
City Attorney

SARAH REYNOSO
Deputy City Attorney

SR/lh

Enclosure

Telecommunications Device for the Deaf (510) 644-6915

DRAFT

MEMORANDUM

March 24, 1992

To:     Assistant City Managers
        and Department Directors

From:   Michael F. Brown, City Manager

Subject: EXECUTIVE COLLATERAL GEOGRAPHICAL RESPONSIBILITY

The Executive Collateral Geographical Responsibility program is designed to
enhance the quality of life in the City.

Assigning key executives to a specific geographical area of responsibility
insures maximum executive oversight of the entire City.  Dividing the City
into 30 areas reduces the burden of one or two individuals having the
responsibility of caring for the entire City.

Each executive will assume responsibility for his/her area.  Many assignments
are located within the areas where executives reside, the intent of the
program is to report on deficiencies in each of the areas.  However, the
Executive Collateral Geographical Responsibility Program is not designed to be
self-serving.  Thus, an executive should report deficiencies near one's home,
however, executives should refrain from using the power of his/her office to
influence personal gain.  Essentially, if a pot-hole exists in front of your
house block, as an E.C.G.R., you should report the problem, but do
not expect the problem to receive favorable treatment.

On the surface it would appear that Public Works will be inundated with
requests for services; however, it is important for executives to observe and
report deficiencies in your area related to all departments.  For example, if
in your area a pile of rubbish is left unattended on a vacant lot, contact the
Fire Prevention Department, or the Environmental Health Department, if a house
has a porch that is leaning, contact Codes and Inspection.  Essentially, in
implementing this program, the participating executives have an opportunity to
report on many activities in the area.  These could be deficient issues that
warrant reporting, while others perhaps could be complimentary for a job well
done!

Assistant City Managers/Department Directors                    March 24, 1992
Executive Collateral Geographical Responsibility              Page Two


When reporting on E-Mail to the responsible department, cc Weldon Rucker, who
will maintain a list of reports.  The executive with the most reports at the
end of the year will receive a prize.  Attached please find a copy of your
designated area.  If you have questions or problems, contact Weldon Rucker at
46580.

The following are assigned areas:

Area  Executive                          Area  Executive

      Denise Johnston                          Manuela Albuquerque
      Oscar Sung                              Peggy Kirihara
      Regina Minudri                          Anna Rabkin
      Ron Brown                               Roy Meisner
      Robbie Goodwin                          Neil Mayer
      Patrick Keilch                          Michael Brown
      Vicki Elmer                             Dan Salter
      Gil Kelley                              Don Coykendall
      Jordan Rich                             Jim Polk
      Kathy Flood                             Sonali Bose
      Ed Lau                                  Hal Cronkite
      Steve Belcher                           Gary Bard
      Weldon Rucker                           Landon Williams
      Carmen Nevarez                          Dash Butler
      Phil Kamlarz                            Gary Cates
      Bob Bailey

Attachment
